title: OFFICE OF CIVIL RIGHTS - 211info
text: UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES OFFICE OF CIVIL RIGHTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information about enforcement of laws against discrimination based on race, color, national origin, disability, age, religion, and sex (including pregnancy, sexual orientation, and gender identity) by health care and human services. Also provides information, enforcement, and a complaint process for HIPPA for individuals and professionals. 200 Independence Avenue SW Washington, DC 20201 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week - (800) 368-1019 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Toll-free (800) 368-1019 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Nationwide Services: Government Complaints/Government Ombudsman Offices * Civil Rights Issues Government Information Services * Civil Rights Issues HIPAA Complaints Other Services Offered At This Location: INFORMATION SERVICES - CHILD WELFARE INFORMATION GATEWAY LAW ENFORCEMENT SERVICES - STOPBULLYING.GOV SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - COVID-19 THERAPEUTICS LOCATOR If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES
